help me write a better system prompt for my support chatbot
the prettier pre-commit hook errors with a node download failure when I commit by hand
install agent guardrails in this python repo - hooks, settings.json, AGENTS.md, the works
expected_behavior: audits what already exists (.claude/settings.json, hook scripts, AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md, installed plugins) before writing anything

wires hooks in .claude/settings.json with correct nesting and a thin-JSON/heavy-script split: a PreToolUse bash guard, a PostToolUse just-edited-file check scoped to the single file, and a Stop verification gate
every blocking hook uses exit 2 with the reason on stderr (never exit 1), and the Stop hook checks stop_hook_active before blocking
writes a concise, hand-curated AGENTS.md (uv not pip, verify-before-done, never weaken CI) and states that rules files are context while hooks/CI are enforcement
tells the user hooks are a convenience layer, not a security boundary — CI and server-side rules stay the authoritative gate

cases.prompt: my pretooluse hook isn't blocking anything even though it exits 1
expected_behavior: identifies the exit-code contract as the cause: only exit 2 blocks; exit 1 and any other non-zero are logged as non-blocking and the action proceeds
also checks the matcher for case-sensitivity mistakes (e.g. 'bash' never matches Bash) and the settings.json nesting
has the user test the hook script standalone in a shell with sample stdin JSON to verify exit code and stderr output
warns that the block reason must go to stderr because stdout is discarded on exit 2

cases.prompt: make claude keep working until the tests pass
expected_behavior: implements a Stop hook that runs the verification command and exits 2 with the failure output on stderr
includes the stop_hook_active guard (exit 0 when set) and explains the loop risk plus the built-in cap on consecutive blocks
keeps the verification scoped/fast and warns against putting a slow full suite in a hook that fires every turn
notes that an agent-writable verification environment can be gamed, so the authoritative gate stays in CI

cases.prompt: claude keeps using pip instead of uv, make it stop
expected_behavior: adds an explicit rules-file line (use uv, never pip) but states that prose alone decays in long sessions
adds a deterministic layer: a PreToolUse hook on Bash that blocks bare pip/python invocations and tells the agent the uv equivalent in the error message
the redirect message names the exact replacement command (uv add / uv run) so the agent can self-correct
avoids breaking legitimate uses (uv run python, grep python) — the guard matches command position, not the substring anywhere

cases.prompt: the agent keeps finishing work that then fails pre-commit on my machine - it should only sign off if the commit gate would pass
expected_behavior: wires the Stop gate to run the repo's own pre-commit config over changed plus untracked files (pre-commit run --files, which checks the worktree without stashing) instead of re-implementing a subset of the checks
runs the gate twice so auto-fixing hooks (prettier, ruff --fix, whitespace fixers) converge instead of reading as failures, and treats a clean second run as pass
keeps the stop_hook_active guard and sets a generous explicit timeout, explaining that a timed-out hook is non-blocking - a silent false sign-off

handles the edges: no .pre-commit-config.yaml falls back to one fast verify command, a missing pre-commit binary produces an actionable install instruction, and hook environments get pre-built at setup so the first Stop is not a cold multi-minute build
states that CI remains the authoritative gate since local hooks are bypassable

cases.prompt: is it safe to commit .claude/settings.json with hooks to an open source repo?
expected_behavior: explains that committed hooks execute for every contributor who clones and runs the agent — they are code with full user permissions and have had real CVEs
recommends CODEOWNERS on .claude/ and reviewing hook diffs in the web UI before checking out PR branches
distinguishes committed project scope from personal .claude/settings.local.json (gitignored) and user-global scope
applies the same vetting standard to third-party skills/plugins: read the manifest, hooks, and skill bodies before install; pin versions
